definition: The first-jhāna formula pair marking the continuing presence of vitakka and vicāra.
notes: Use this record for the standard first-jhāna factor pair. Keep the line practical and continuous with the live vitakka and vicāra headword policies rather than turning it into scholastic jargon or anti-discursive mysticism.

example_phrases.notes: Standard first-jhāna factor pair in the entry formula.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English factor pair in first-jhāna entry formulas unless a recorded local reason requires minor grammatical adjustment.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not turn the pair into scholastic labels such as applied and sustained thought, and do not mystify it into anti-thought rhetoric.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Keeps the first-jhāna factor pair aligned with the live vitakka and vicāra policies and with the practical reading of the first mental theme.
